rubric_identity.one_sentence_purpose: Evaluate candidate CVs for AI Architect roles by verifying enterprise AI platform design, production delivery, secure integration, and stakeholder translation evidence while penalizing management inflation and unsupported claims.
rubric_identity.core_persona: Senior individual contributor or player-coach architect who designs enterprise AI platforms, ships production systems with guardrails and evaluation, integrates GenAI into existing enterprise estates, and translates technical tradeoffs for stakeholders — not a people manager, research scientist, or executive.
job_overlay_notes: Adjust provider emphasis (Azure/AWS/GCP) only when JD explicitly requires platform-specific delivery evidence
Weight consulting-style stakeholder translation higher for consultancy or enterprise services JDs
Increase agents/orchestration weight when JD mentions agentic systems, MCP, or workflow automation
Weight compliance signals (GDPR, EU AI Act) only when JD explicitly requires regulatory delivery experience
Reduce modernization weight for pure greenfield-focused JDs; increase for transformation or legacy estate roles

what_good_looks_like: Headline uses architect-family titles (AI Solution Architect, AI Architect, Generative AI Solution Architect) matching 87% of category title distribution
Platform design, solution architecture, enterprise integration keywords appear 2-3 times across headline/summary/experience
RAG, agents, guardrails, evaluation keywords present only when backed by shipped work evidence
Python, LangChain, MLOps, Kubernetes appear as supporting evidence not headline anchors since no hard skill exceeds 36%
Stakeholder alignment, cross-functional collaboration language appears in summary given 76% communication signal
common_failures: Headline anchors on single tech keyword (Python, LangChain) rather than architecture outcomes
Uses management titles (Head of AI, Director, VP) when category is 79% senior IC
Claims multi-cloud or provider-specific authority without delivery evidence for that platform
Overloads skills section with framework lists without tying to architecture outcomes
Missing guardrails/governance keywords despite 37.5% category frequency

what_to_adjust_per_jd: If JD emphasizes Azure/AWS: verify CV shows named delivery on that platform before accepting provider keywords
If JD mentions agents/MCP: weight agent orchestration keywords higher in evaluation
If JD requires German/Polish: verify native language claims before penalizing missing language keywords
company_domain_overrides: Consulting firms: increase weight for client-facing, advisory, workshop language
Enterprise SaaS: increase weight for multi-tenant, platform rollout, scale keywords
Regulated industries: accept compliance keywords (GDPR, SOC2) only with delivery evidence
region_overrides: EU roles: GDPR/EU AI Act keywords acceptable only with explicit compliance delivery evidence
DACH region: German language requirements vary; verify language evidence before penalizing

9_10: Headline uses architect-family title with enterprise AI platform, integration, and governance keywords
Keywords distributed naturally across sections with 2-3 mentions of core signals (architecture, delivery, stakeholders)
No keyword stuffing; every technical keyword maps to evidenced shipped work
Provider/framework keywords appear only where CV proves delivery on that stack
7_8: Headline uses appropriate architect title but keyword distribution slightly unbalanced
Most core signals present but one or two (guardrails, evaluation, agents) underrepresented
Some framework keywords lack direct tie to architecture outcomes
Provider keywords present with partial delivery evidence
5_6: Headline acceptable but missing key differentiators (governance, production delivery)
Core architecture keywords present but buried in skills section rather than experience
Some keyword stuffing or over-reliance on single technical stack
Missing stakeholder/communication keywords despite category emphasis
0_4: Headline uses inflated titles (Head of AI, Director) or wrong family (Data Scientist, ML Engineer)
Missing core architecture keywords; reads as generic developer or manager CV
Heavy keyword stuffing without evidence tie or framework list without outcomes
Claims multi-cloud/provider authority without any delivery evidence

red_flags: Headline claims Head of AI, VP, or Director without supporting management evidence
Multi-cloud or provider-specific authority claims without named platform delivery
Research Architect or PhD-adjacent framing when category shows 0% PhD requirement
Framework list exceeds 10 items without architecture outcome tie-ins
Missing all of: architecture, platform design, solution architect keywords

what_good_looks_like: Architecture achievements lead with platform scope (users served, business units, tenants) before tech details
Production delivery metrics quantified: uptime/SLO, deployment frequency, time-to-production
Scale and reliability outcomes explicit: throughput increase, latency reduction, incident reduction percentages
Cost efficiency quantified where applicable: infrastructure savings, token cost reduction, maintenance effort reduced
Integration achievements show systems connected, workflows automated, manual effort eliminated
common_failures: Architecture claims without production delivery proof (slideware not shipped systems)
Generic platform descriptions without scope quantification (users, scale, reliability)
Tech stack lists without outcome metrics; reads as resume not impact statement
Missing cost, latency, or reliability metrics when those were achievable outcomes
Guardrails/evaluation mentioned without quality or error reduction metrics

what_to_adjust_per_jd: If JD emphasizes cost optimization: weight cost reduction and efficiency metrics higher
If JD mentions enterprise scale: expect user counts, tenant counts, or transaction volumes
If JD focuses on security/compliance: weight risk reduction and audit outcomes
company_domain_overrides: Consulting: accept engagement scope metrics (clients served, projects delivered) alongside platform metrics
Startups: accept growth trajectory metrics; absolute scale may be smaller
Enterprise: expect larger absolute numbers; percentages alone may be insufficient
region_overrides: Global/remote roles: accept diverse engagement contexts; no single region expectation

9_10: Every major achievement quantified with scope (2000+ users, 42 plugins, billions of events)
Architecture decisions tied to measurable production outcomes (75% incident reduction, 3 years zero downtime)
Cost, latency, reliability, and scale metrics all present where applicable
STAR/ARIS format clear: constraint → decision → outcome with numbers
7_8: Most achievements quantified but one or two lack specific metrics
Production outcomes clear but some scope metrics missing (users, scale)
Cost or latency metrics present for main achievements but not all
Story structure clear but some bullets read as task lists not outcomes
5_6: Some quantification present but inconsistent across achievements
Architecture scope implied but not explicitly quantified
Production delivery mentioned but outcomes vague (improved performance vs 40% faster)
Mix of outcome-focused and task-focused bullets
0_4: No meaningful quantification; all achievements described qualitatively
Architecture claims without any production evidence
Reads as task list or responsibility dump not impact statement
Metrics present but clearly fabricated or implausible

red_flags: Platform claims without user count, scale, or production deployment evidence
Architecture outcomes described only as potential not shipped
Metrics that seem fabricated or inconsistent with role scope
Guardrails or evaluation mentioned without quality improvement metrics
Cost savings claimed without mechanism or baseline explanation

what_good_looks_like: Platform design evidence present (98.9% category signal) with architecture ownership explicit
Production delivery proof with shipped systems not just designs or proposals
GenAI production stack evidence: agents/orchestration (38.6%), RAG (28.4%), guardrails (37.5%), evaluation (20.5%)
Enterprise integration patterns shown: microservices, APIs, service boundaries
Stakeholder translation evidence: cross-functional collaboration, decision framing, workshops
common_failures: Missing platform design evidence when it appears in 98.9% of category JDs
Heavy on model/ML details but light on enterprise integration and architecture
No guardrails or governance evidence despite 37.5% category frequency
Stakeholder collaboration absent despite 76% communication signal
Research or experimentation focus when category shows 0% research-heavy signal

what_to_adjust_per_jd: If JD requires specific cloud (Azure/AWS): must see named delivery on that platform
If JD emphasizes agents/MCP: weight orchestration and tool integration evidence higher
If JD mentions specific frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph): verify those appear in CV with outcomes
If JD requires compliance expertise: look for explicit regulatory delivery evidence
company_domain_overrides: Consulting/advisory: increase weight for client-facing architecture, proposal-to-delivery continuity
Product company: increase weight for platform ownership, roadmap influence, cross-team collaboration
Regulated enterprise: require explicit compliance delivery evidence not just awareness
region_overrides: German/DACH JDs with language requirements: verify German C1+ claim has evidence
EU JDs with GDPR emphasis: require hands-on compliance delivery not just mentions

9_10: All category must-haves covered: platform design, production delivery, enterprise integration
GenAI stack evidence matches JD emphasis (agents, RAG, guardrails, evaluation as needed)
Stakeholder translation and collaboration evidence explicit
Any JD-specific requirements (cloud platform, framework, domain) directly evidenced
7_8: Core must-haves present but one category differentiator (agents, evaluation, guardrails) light
GenAI evidence present but depth varies across stack components
Stakeholder evidence present but not prominent
Most JD-specific requirements met but one or two gaps
5_6: Platform design present but production delivery evidence thin
GenAI stack partially covered; missing agents or evaluation evidence
Stakeholder collaboration implied but not explicit
Some JD-specific requirements unmet or weakly evidenced
0_4: Missing platform design or architecture ownership evidence
No production delivery proof; reads as experimental or research-focused
GenAI evidence generic or absent
Major JD requirements unmet (wrong cloud, missing domain, no stakeholder evidence)

red_flags: Research or PhD framing when category shows 0% research-heavy signal
Management-first positioning when category is 79% senior IC
Claims customer-facing or external workshop experience without evidence
Provider-specific authority (Azure/AWS) without named delivery examples
Full-stack or frontend emphasis when category centers on platform architecture

what_good_looks_like: Architectural authority demonstrated through platform ownership, design decisions, and system governance
Technical translation skill shown: explaining architecture tradeoffs to business stakeholders
Player-coach evidence: mentoring engineers, leading design reviews, guiding architectural patterns
Enterprise influence through cross-functional collaboration not org-chart authority
Production ownership: accountable for system quality, reliability, and operational outcomes
common_failures: Claims executive leadership, C-suite access, or boardroom presence without evidence
Describes people management (hiring, performance reviews) when category shows 2% signal
Uses Head of, Director, or VP framing without supporting management scope
Org-building or team-scaling language without explicit evidence
Generic leadership adjectives (strategic, visionary) without architectural substance

what_to_adjust_per_jd: If JD mentions tech lead: look for design review ownership and pattern guidance
If JD emphasizes client-facing: require explicit stakeholder workshop or advisory evidence
If JD mentions player-coach: weight mentoring and coaching evidence higher
company_domain_overrides: Consulting: accept client-facing architecture authority as executive presence
Startup: accept broader scope with smaller team; influence matters more than org size
Enterprise: expect cross-functional collaboration evidence across larger org
region_overrides: No significant regional variation for architectural authority interpretation

9_10: Clear platform or system ownership with architectural decision authority
Stakeholder translation explicit: workshops, decision framing, cross-functional alignment
Player-coach evidence: mentoring engineers, design reviews, pattern guidance (not people management)
Production accountability demonstrated through reliability, quality, and delivery outcomes
7_8: Architectural ownership clear but stakeholder translation evidence light
Some mentoring or guidance evidence but not prominent
Technical authority evident but cross-functional collaboration underrepresented
Production outcomes present but ownership framing could be stronger
5_6: Architectural work evident but ownership ambiguous (contributor vs owner)
Stakeholder interaction mentioned but translation skill not demonstrated
Team context present but no mentoring or guidance evidence
Production involvement clear but accountability framing missing
0_4: No architectural ownership evidence; reads as implementer not architect
Claims management authority (Head of, Director) without supporting scope
Executive or C-suite language without architectural substance
People management emphasis when category shows 2% hiring signal

red_flags: Unsupported people-management framing (hiring ownership, performance reviews, org building)
C-suite, boardroom, or executive leadership claims without evidence
Head of AI, Director, VP titles without management scope evidence
P&L, budget ownership, or business strategy claims unrelated to architecture
Generic leadership buzzwords (visionary, strategic leader) without technical substance

what_good_looks_like: All metrics traceable to specific roles, projects, or systems named in CV
Titles match evidenced responsibilities; no inflation to Director/VP/Head without scope
Cloud/platform claims (AWS, Azure) tied to named delivery examples
Framework and tool claims tied to architecture outcomes not just skill lists
Compliance claims (GDPR, EU AI Act) backed by explicit delivery evidence
common_failures: Metrics appear without context (saved $2M but no baseline or mechanism explained)
PhD, publication, or research framing when category shows 0% research requirement
Multi-cloud authority claimed without delivery evidence on multiple platforms
Customer-facing or advisory experience claimed without stakeholder evidence
Scope inflation: small team project described as enterprise platform

what_to_adjust_per_jd: If JD requires specific provider: verify CV shows delivery on that provider not just mentions
If JD requires domain expertise: verify domain experience is evidenced not claimed
If JD requires compliance expertise: verify regulatory delivery evidence not awareness
company_domain_overrides: Consulting: verify client-facing claims match evidenced engagement types
Regulated industries: scrutinize compliance claims for delivery evidence
Enterprise: verify scale claims match evidenced org context
region_overrides: German language C1+ claims: verify through evidence or flag as unverifiable
EU AI Act expertise: require explicit delivery evidence not just awareness

9_10: Every metric tied to named project, system, or role with plausible mechanism
Titles accurately reflect evidenced responsibilities; no inflation
All platform/framework claims backed by delivery examples
No unsupported compliance, domain, or scope claims
7_8: Most metrics well-sourced but one or two lack clear project tie
Titles appropriate but one role description slightly inflated
Platform claims supported but one framework claim lacks outcome tie
Minor scope ambiguity in one achievement
5_6: Some metrics present without clear source or mechanism
Title appropriate but responsibility description somewhat inflated
Some platform claims lack delivery evidence
Scope of one or two achievements unclear
0_4: Multiple metrics appear fabricated or implausible for described role
Title clearly inflated (Head of AI, VP) without management evidence
Multi-cloud, PhD, or research claims without any supporting evidence
Significant scope inflation or outright hallucinated achievements

red_flags: Unsourced metrics: percentages, dollar amounts, or user counts without project context
Fabricated titles: Director, VP, Head without management scope evidence
PhD or publication claims when CV shows no academic background
Multi-cloud authority without named delivery on multiple providers
DevOps or infrastructure ownership claims when evidence shows architecture collaboration only
Customer-facing, advisory, or workshop facilitation claims without stakeholder evidence
GDPR, EU AI Act, or compliance program ownership without regulatory delivery proof
Claims of reports, direct reports, or team size without supporting evidence

pass_criteria: Platform design or solution architecture ownership explicitly evidenced
Production AI delivery proof (shipped system not just design or proposal)
Enterprise integration or distributed systems evidence present
At least one GenAI production signal evidenced: agents, RAG, guardrails, or evaluation
fail_conditions: No platform design or architecture ownership evidence
No production delivery proof; all work is experimental, POC, or proposal
No enterprise integration pattern evidence (pure model work only)
Zero GenAI production signals despite category emphasis

pass_criteria: All cloud/platform claims tied to named delivery examples
Titles match evidenced responsibilities without inflation
Metrics traceable to specific projects with plausible mechanisms
No unsupported management, research, or compliance program ownership claims
fail_conditions: Multi-cloud authority claimed without multi-platform delivery evidence
Director, VP, or Head titles without management scope evidence
PhD, publication, or research emphasis when CV shows no academic background
Compliance program ownership (GDPR, EU AI Act) claimed without regulatory delivery evidence
Customer-facing or workshop facilitation claimed without stakeholder evidence
DevOps or infrastructure ownership claimed when evidence shows collaboration only

pass_criteria: Presents as senior IC or player-coach architect, not people manager or executive
Architecture and delivery outcomes foreground, management and org-building background
Production systems orientation, not research or experimentation focus
Stakeholder translation framed as technical collaboration not business strategy
fail_conditions: Presents as people manager (hiring ownership, performance reviews, org building) without evidence
Research or academic positioning when category shows 0% PhD/research requirement
Executive or C-suite framing without architectural substance
Full-stack developer or frontend emphasis when category centers on platform architecture

scoring_guidance.how_to_interpret_executive_presence: For AI Architect category, executive_presence means architectural authority and system ownership: platform design decisions, production accountability, stakeholder translation, and player-coach mentoring. It does NOT mean C-suite access, boardroom presence, P&L ownership, or formal management hierarchy. Score based on technical influence and ownership, not org-chart seniority. Penalize claims of people management (hiring, performance reviews) unless explicitly evidenced, since category shows only 2.3% hiring signal.
scoring_guidance.how_to_penalize_unsupported_claims: Deduct 0.5-1.0 points per unsupported claim in anti_hallucination dimension. Unsupported claims include: metrics without project context, title inflation beyond evidenced scope, multi-cloud authority without multi-platform delivery, PhD/research framing without academic evidence, compliance ownership without regulatory delivery proof, customer-facing claims without stakeholder evidence. Claims that fail the unsafe_claim_gate should result in maximum 5.0 score regardless of other merits.
scoring_guidance.how_to_handle_category_vs_job_tradeoffs: Category rubric establishes baseline expectations (platform design 98.9%, architecture evidence, senior IC persona). Job-specific requirements layer on top: if JD requires Azure and CV shows only AWS, penalize in jd_alignment but not in category_core signals. When JD requirements conflict with category norms (e.g., JD asks for management focus), flag the conflict but evaluate against JD requirements since hiring decision serves specific role needs.

evidence_ledger.rubric_rule: Platform design is table-stakes requirement appearing in 98.9% of category roles
Architecture valued evidence type appears in 20/20 deep analyses making it highest signal
Category is 79% senior IC with only 2.3% hiring signal; penalize management inflation
Agents/orchestration appears in 38.6% of roles as top GenAI production signal
Guardrails/governance appears in 37.5% of roles indicating production control expectation
Communication appears in 76.1% and stakeholder management in 44.3% requiring translation evidence
Research-heavy and PhD requirements are 0% making academic framing unsafe
Azure and AWS each appear in only 15.9% so provider authority requires explicit delivery evidence
Safe titles are solutions_architect (50%) and ai_architect (37%); avoid Head/Director/VP inflation
Baseline shows 7.8 combined fit score for candidate with strong Commander-4 AI platform evidence
RAG pipeline evidence with hybrid search and evaluation harness matches 28.4% RAG and 20.5% evaluation signals
Player-coach mentoring evidence supports 38% player_coach signal without inflating into management
